title: light briny vietnamese pork shoulder fry
description: a light and refreshing vietnamese-inspired dish featuring pork shoulder, perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: in a small bowl, whisk together fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, and 2 cloves of minced garlic.
in a large skillet or wok, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
add the pork shoulder to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until browned.
remove the pork from the skillet and set aside.
in the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil and cook for 1 minute.
add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add the soy sauce and rice vinegar to the skillet, whisking to combine.
return the pork shoulder to the skillet and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until coated in the sauce.
transfer the pork to a cutting board and let rest for 5 minutes.
slice the pork into thin strips and serve with green onions and fresh cilantro scattered on top.
